reason: Despite significant ETF inflows, Bitcoin's price is not appreciating, indicating strong selling pressure and potential distribution from 'smart money' absorbing retail demand. Elevated geopolitical risks are driving a broad risk-off sentiment, evidenced by sliding altcoins and traditional equities. While Bitcoin shows relative resilience by 'holding,' this masks an underlying weakness that could lead to a markdown if macro conditions deteriorate further.
headlines: Ray Dalio says 'there is only one gold' as bitcoin outperforms gold during worst geopolitical crisis in years
Over a billion flows into bitcoin ETFs, yet the price isn’t rising — an analyst explains why
Bitcoin holds, ether, solana slide as Mideast woes drag Asian equities to multi-year lows
Ripple expands payments platform into end-to-end stablecoin infrastructure as processed volume tops $100 billion
Polymarket shelves nuclear detonation markets after outcry
